Welcome to Databricks Field Engineering! We are looking forward to having you in Flight School at Databricks HQ in San Francisco. 

Flight School is a 3.5 day training event for new Field Eng Bricksters in customer-facing roles to learn and apply skills integral to their new role. The week will culminate in a Capstone Project where new Bricksters will collaborate and deliver a final presentation. 

CLASS LOCATION: 

Databricks HQ - 3rd Floor "Monstropolis" Room
160 Spear Street
San Francisco, CA, USA

AIR TRAVEL:

Airfare must be booked in Egencia, adhering to our global expense policy.
*If you experience an issue booking travel, contact #travelandexpense_support
**For visa assistance, please reach out to the Global Mobility Team

HOTELS:

Lodging must be booked in Egencia, adhering to our global expense policy. 

Bricksters commonly stay at the following locations:

  • San Francisco Marriott Marquis - 780 Mission St, San Francisco, CA 94103
  • Hyatt Regency San Francisco - 5 Embarcadero Ctr, San Francisco, CA 94111
  • Harbor Court Hotel - 165 Steuart St, San Francisco, CA 94105

ATTIRE:

Business Casual (jeans are perfectly acceptable👍)

IMPORTANT REMINDERS: 

  1. Submit for a badge via Freshservice, if you do not have one. If you have a badge, bring it with you for building access. 
  2. Please bring your laptop, you will need it throughout the week. 
  3. Meals: 
    • Breakfast: on your own. Breakfast will not be provided, but feel free to grab breakfast at your hotel, or on your way into the office. 
    • Lunch: 1 hour break each day for lunch - Request access to Forkable
    • to order meals for delivery to SF HQ. 


International Bricksters: Plan to arrive two days before the training start date

AMER Bricksters: Plan to arrive one day before the training start date

Departures: Flight School concludes at 12 p.m. local time on Day 4. You can not leave early. You will not receive credit if you depart early.

summary: Databricks/3M Corporate Research Labs open office hours
Level Up [Hands On Lab]: Lakebase Core Concepts
description:
Join the new Level Up - Hands On Workshop series to gain hands on expertise on curated labs.


Topic: : Lakebase Core Concepts

Instructor: Peter Styliadis

➡️ Prerequisite:
To follow along hands on during the session need the following prior to attending:
  • Access to a workspace that has Lakebase Provisioned enabled. Each workspace can support up to 10 Lakebase instances, and if you are on a shared workspace it might already be near the limit, which can restrict creating new instances.If you want your own temporary workspace, create one on go/fevm.
  • Ability to CREATE A CATALOG in your Workspace.
Please do this prior to the start of the session.

➡️ Session Details:
This session introduces Databricks Lakebase, a fully managed PostgreSQL service that bridges operational (OLTP) and analytical (OLAP) workloads within the unified Databricks Data Intelligence Platform.

Through a combination of lectures and hands-on labs, you'll cover the fundamentals of OLTP vs OLAP concepts, Lakebase architecture and integration patterns, multiple database connection methods, and practical implementation of reverse ETL workflows. Participants will gain hands-on experience creating Lakebase instances, establishing Python connections, implementing CRUD operations, and synchronizing Unity Catalog tables to operational databases (Reverse ETL).

By the end, you'll understand how Lakebase unifies transactional and analytical systems through Unity Catalog and Lakehouse Federation, enabling governed, real-time applications that bridge the gap between data analytics and data operations.

summary: Level Up: Accelerate and Unblock Migrations with Multi-Statement, Multi-Table Transactions
description:
Join the Level Up series to dive deep into the product features, roadmap, demo and limitations. Bring all your customer questions for the AMA segment.

Topic - Accelerate and Unblock Migrations with Multi-Statement, Multi-Table Transactions


Speakers - Michelle Leon. Benjamin Mathew & Ambarish Dongaonkar

💡 Agenda 
Multi-Statement and Multi-Table Transactions (MSTs) are the most-requested Databricks SQL feature, providing atomic and reliable updates across several tables at once. MSTs are set to become production-ready by November 2025, enabling the field to unblock ~$100M in stalled migration and ETL workloads for enterprise customers.

What Will Be Covered
  • MST essentials: how atomic transactions work across multiple tables in Databricks
  • Real-world use cases: multi-table updates, audit logging, migration patterns, deduplication strategies
  • Key technical semantics, implementation steps, and getting started with MSTs
  • Live demos and Q&A on onboarding customers

Meeting Link: https://databricks.zoom.us/j/82759528384

LAKEHOUSE FOUNDATIONS PART 3 - ARCHITECTURE PILLARS & ESSENTIALS

KEY DETAILS:

	* When: January 28th, 2026 12:00 PM–1:00 PM CT
	*
Format: Virtual

	* Databricks Speakers: Priyanka Kasliwal, Andres Zuñiga
	*
Target Audience: Platform Architects, Data Architects, Platform Admins

	*
Short description: Design a well‑run Lakehouse from day one. In this session, we’ll use the Databricks Well‑Architected Framework to meet security & compliance, optimize TCO, unlock scalability, and simplify operations—with a live whiteboarding walkthrough that visualizes control vs. compute, regional deployment, and serverless vs. classic compute.

VALUE TO ATTENDEES & TOPICS: (Why you should attend)

	* The DATABRICKS WELL‑ARCHITECTED FRAMEWORK pillars and how to apply them. 
	* How CONTROL, COMPUTE PLANE separation enables flexibility and security. 
	* GEOGRAPHIC DEPLOYMENT basics: region selection, data locality, and residency requirements. 
	* How ACCOUNTS → REGIONS → WORKSPACES → METASTORES shape governance and operations. 
	* Cross‑geo considerations: Navigating LOCAL REGULATIONS. 
	* How SERVERLESS VS. CLASSIC compute impacts performance and cost. 
	* Mapping DATABRICKS COMPUTE to cloud VM families (e.g., EC2, Azure VMs) for analytics, streaming, and AI.

We are running back our #toandthrough planning process to give all of us visibility into our path to run through our targets this year. During this call, we'll make sure you all are clear on the rationale, the process, and timelines for completing this across your territories.

This process enables every account team (AE + SA) and leader to plot their course for a successful FY27. All teams will analyze their territories and develop a plan to achieve and beat their FY27 consumption target, specifically across 3 dimensions:

GROW THE BASELINE - What will the organic growth rate be for my territory?  How can we maximize this?

EXECUTE USE CASE PIPE - What use cases will land this year?  What can I accelerate?

FIND NEW USE CASES & TAM -What whitespace exists for us to capture - new business line?  New product offering?

We will schedule follow up office hours and send slides and recording after the session.

summary: Level Up: Data Warehousing - LakeBridge Migrations
description:
Topic: Data Warehousing - LakeBridge Migrations
Presenter: Dan Davis 


Meeting Link: https://databricks.zoom.us/j/82759528384

This Level Up session on DWH LakeBridge migrations will equip you to confidently deliver the standardized, hands-on LakeBridge Migration Workshop as a pre-sales accelerator for data warehouse modernization. You’ll learn how to position the workshop, use the latest LakeBridge tooling (including the GenAI-based “switch”), and drive clear post-workshop next steps that build migration momentum.

What Will Be Covered  
  1. How to position the LakeBridge Workshop as a pre-sales discovery and migration catalyst. 
  2. Core tooling: Databricks Assistant, LakeBridge Analyzer, and LakeBridge Converter (BladeBridge Transpiler + “switch” GenAI).  
  3. Step-by-step hands on demo flow using standardized Synapse SQL and Informatica ETL scripts, plus the recommended post-workshop follow-up plan.
  4. Guided next steps including customer call to action running the Analyzer and initiating an STS engagement.

Attend the workshop to:
  • Clearly articulate the value and scope of the LakeBridge Workshop versus an STS engagement.  
  • Confidently run the end-to-end, CLI-based workshop using the provided demo repo and sample source code.  
  • Use the workshop outputs to guide customers toward Analyzer runs and STS engagements, framing migrations as frictionless and achievable.


Access the recording and material post session here.

description:

Topic: DBX-UnifiedChat

SME Presenters: Yang Yang, Brennan Beal, Eli Swanson, May Merkle-Tan

Meeting Link: https://databricks.zoom.us/j/82759528384


Join this 2-hour technical level-up session to dive deep into DBX-UnifiedChat, a production-ready, multi-agent system built on the Databricks Platform. 

This session will explore how to leverage LangGraph, Databricks Genie, Lakebase, and Claude models to enable business users to ask natural language questions spanning multiple data domains without needing to understand complex schemas or write a single line of SQL. We will unpack the system's 7-agent orchestration architecture, the automated Tables-to-Genies Databricks App, and the underlying ETL pipelines that power intelligent semantic routing and multi-step instructed retrieval.

Key Takeaways

  • Automated Genie Space Creation: Discover how the "Tables-to-Genies" Databricks App uses a 5-step wizard and graph analysis to transform raw Unity Catalog tables into intelligent Genie spaces.

  • Metadata Enrichment ETL: Learn how the enrichment pipeline prepares high-quality semantic context for the agents.

  • Multi-Agent Orchestration: Understand the LangGraph-powered architecture featuring 7 specialized agents that achieves ultra-fast 1–2s TTFT and completes complex queries in ~1/2 the time of standard AB-MAS solutions.

  • Supervisor (Frontdoor) Agent Deep Dive: Explore a specialized subgraph agent node with structured output tools designed to lift classification precision, accelerate speed, and reduce token costs.

  • Cross-Domain Querying & Synthesis: Learn how the system concurrently routes queries across multiple Databricks Genie spaces and synthesizes cross-domain SQL to deliver accurate, curated answers.

  • Advanced Retrieval & Memory: See how to implement Multi-Step Instructed Retrieval (acting as a "detective" to collect clues) and maintain persistent context.

  • Deployment and Playground Benchmark: Review the deployment workflow and performance benchmarking against Agent Bricks MAS (AB MAS).


Agenda

  • The Vision: Cross-Domain Intelligence on Databricks (15 mins)

  • The Ingredients: Metadata Enrichment & ETL Pipelines (15 mins)

  • The Factory: Automated Tables-to-Genies App (Dash + NetworkX) (15 mins)

  • The Brain: LangGraph Orchestration & Supervisor Deep Dive (20 mins)

  • The Execution: Parallel Genie Routing & SQL Synthesis (20 mins)

  • The Memory: Lakebase Persistence & Instructed Retrieval (15 mins)

  • The Delivery: Deployment & AB-MAS Benchmarking (10 mins)

  • Q&A and Level-Up Roadmap (10 mins)
